data.body:
ROUND 8 — structured-output reliability + concurrency decision + EPIC SPLIT.

STRUCTURED-OUTPUT RELIABILITY (Joe: how do popular Pydantic projects guarantee schema-conforming output;
retire the second-interpreter LLM). RESEARCH VERDICT: the second-interpreter LLM IS a recognized anti-pattern
(can't validate non-determinism with more non-determinism). Converged LAYERED stack:
1. Provider-native CONSTRAINED decoding / strict json_schema where supported — OpenAI strict ~100% vs <40%
   unconstrained; Gemini responseSchema; **Anthropic native structured outputs now GA (constrained, compiled
   grammar; output_config.format + strict tools)** — via Pydantic AI NativeOutput; else PromptedOutput cross-
   provider. (JSONSchemaBench: native constraint ~96-100% on easy/mid schemas, collapses on HARD schemas;
   constraining IMPROVED accuracy +4%, contra "Let Me Speak Freely" which itself used a 2nd-LLM parser a regex beat.)
2. Deterministic TOLERANT parse of near-miss output: json-repair (~5k*, pure Python) — no LLM, no retry. (BAML
   schema-aligned-parsing is the richer version but is a separate .baml DSL.)
3. Pydantic validation + normalizing validators — put numeric/length bounds HERE, not in the JSON Schema, to
   stay inside Anthropic's strict-grammar SUBSET (no recursion, no numeric/length bounds, <=20 strict tools).
4. Bounded SAME-model retry feeding the ValidationError back (Pydantic AI retries={'output':2}; DEFAULT budget
   is 1 -> raise it). Instructor (~13k*) does the same (re-ask with the error via Tenacity).
ANTHROPIC SPECIFICS: handle stop_reason in {refusal, max_tokens} (can break schema). Extended thinking is a
TRADEOFF vs structured output (incompatible with FORCED tool use; thinking+native-JSON undocumented) -> for
steps needing thinking, SPLIT reasoning and structured extraction into TWO steps. NET: layers 1-4 retire the
second-interpreter LLM deterministically. (Constrained-decoding libs Outlines/XGrammar/llguidance are local-
model-only — they are the engines BEHIND the provider APIs; not usable for hosted closed models.)
Report saved: docs/experiments/workflow-remediation-pocs/structured-output-research.md (relocated from repo root).

CONCURRENCY: DECISION RECORDED — bounded-concurrent fan-out in v1 (parallel LLM calls, serialized event
commits through the existing lock; deterministic replay preserved by iteration-keyed markers; Burr tripwire
relaxed NARROWLY for the map path only). In e6d9 success+acceptance criteria.

EPIC SPLIT (Joe directive): created NEW epic e6d9-41da-da0d-44ef "hump-seam-spice" (P1) owning the BUILD —
declarative IR + thin interpreter (event-log run-state, bounded-concurrent fan-out) + provider-agnostic
Pydantic AI runtime (replacing LangGraph) + ephemeral bpmn-js visual editing + the structured-output
reliability stack. Full converged design + dependencies + invariant-mapping + 3 POC results + limitations +
success/acceptance criteria captured in the description (check-ac PASS 8 / clarity 7). 6f2d now DEPENDS_ON
e6d9 and RETAINS remediation-on-top (WS-DOC/WS-MIGRATE/WS-PROMPTS/WS-EVAL-EXISTING/WS-CLEANUP/WS-VISUAL-EVAL/
WS-INTENT-ALIGN); WS-VALIDATE resolved = keep-and-rebuild. Split recorded as a 6f2d comment.

WORKING TREE (uncommitted): docs/experiments/workflow-remediation-pocs/ (engine_interpreter_poc.py,
runtime_pydanticai_poc.py, visual_bpmn_roundtrip_poc.mjs, structured-output-research.md) + the
joe-brainstorm SKILL.md "interrogate novelty" addition (~/.claude, not this repo). Not committed (awaiting ask).

STATE: brainstorm core done — architecture designed, de-risked (3/3 POCs), and captured into a new build epic
e6d9 with implementable AC. Next: decompose e6d9 into child stories / start build / commit POCs.
